rules.title: Narrow wildcard permission allow rules
rules.advice: One or more entries in your permissions allow list contain a wildcard (*). Allow rules let Claude Code use the matching tools without asking you first, so a broad pattern can auto-approve far more than you intended.
rules.fix: Run `permissions --audit` to list the overbroad entries, then tighten each rule in settings.json (or via /permissions inside Claude Code) to the narrowest pattern you actually need.
rules.sourceStatement: Allow rules let Claude Code use the specified tool without manual approval. [...] `Bash(*)` is equivalent to `Bash` and matches all Bash commands.

rules.title: Resolve duplicate component names
rules.advice: Two or more of your components resolve to the same name, so Claude Code applies its precedence rules and loads only one of them — the shadowed copies are silently inactive.
rules.fix: Rename one copy, or delete the duplicate you don't want. claude-mgr `remove <kind>:<name>` (kind = skill/agent/command) is dry-run by default and snapshots before deleting, so it is reversible.
rules.sourceStatement: When skills share the same name across levels, enterprise overrides personal, and personal overrides project. If you have files in .claude/commands/, those work the same way.

rules.title: Resolve duplicate subagent names
rules.advice: Two or more subagents share the same name. Claude Code keeps the copy from the higher-priority location and silently ignores the others, so the shadowed definition never runs.
rules.fix: Rename one of the agents, or delete the duplicate (claude-mgr `remove agent:<name>` is dry-run by default and snapshots before deleting).
rules.sourceStatement: When multiple subagents share the same name, the higher-priority location wins. [...] if two files within one scope declare the same name, Claude Code keeps one and discards the other without warning.

rules.title: Reinstall or remove a missing enabled plugin
rules.advice: Your settings enable a plugin that is not actually installed, so none of its skills, agents, hooks, or MCP servers can load.
rules.fix: Reinstall it with /plugin install <name>@<marketplace> inside Claude Code, or remove the stale entry from enabledPlugins in settings.json.
rules.sourceStatement: Plugins extend Claude Code with skills, agents, hooks, and MCP servers. [...] Once you've added marketplaces, you can install plugins directly: /plugin install plugin-name@marketplace-name.

rules.title: Enable or uninstall an inactive plugin
rules.advice: A plugin is installed on disk but not enabled in your settings, so it is dormant — its components do not load.
rules.fix: Enable it with /plugin enable <name>@<marketplace> (then /reload-plugins to apply without restarting), or uninstall it to declutter.
rules.sourceStatement: Run /plugin and go to the Installed tab to view, enable, disable, or uninstall your plugins. [...] Re-enable a disabled plugin: /plugin enable plugin-name@marketplace-name.

rules.title: Rebuild a missing plugin cache
rules.advice: An enabled plugin's cached files are missing from ~/.claude/plugins/cache, so its skills and other components can fail to load until the cache is rebuilt.
rules.fix: Reinstall the plugin from its marketplace (/plugin), or clear the plugin cache and reinstall, then restart Claude Code.
rules.sourceStatement: Plugins are copied to a cache [...] Plugin skills not appearing: Clear the cache with rm -rf ~/.claude/plugins/cache, restart Claude Code, and reinstall the plugin.

rules.title: Re-add the plugin's marketplace
rules.advice: An installed plugin references a marketplace that is not in your known marketplaces, so Claude Code cannot refresh or reinstall that plugin from its source.
rules.fix: Re-add the marketplace with /plugin marketplace add <source>, or reinstall the plugin from a marketplace you still have.
rules.sourceStatement: If Claude Code reports that the plugin is not found in any marketplace, your marketplace is either missing or outdated. Run /plugin marketplace update [...] or /plugin marketplace add [...] Then retry the install.

rules.title: Re-authenticate a stale MCP server
rules.advice: An MCP server has been flagged as needing authentication for a long time, which usually means its OAuth login expired or was never completed — the server's tools won't work until you sign in again.
rules.fix: Run /mcp inside Claude Code and complete the OAuth flow for that server (the /mcp panel lets you manage and re-authenticate that server).
rules.sourceStatement: Claude Code marks a remote server as needing authentication when the server responds with 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden. Either status code flags the server in /mcp so you can complete the OAuth flow.

rules.title: Fix an unresolvable MCP server command
rules.advice: A stdio MCP server's launch command could not be found on PATH when probed. Stdio servers run as local processes, so the server may fail to start (the probe's PATH can differ from Claude Code's launch environment).
rules.fix: Install the missing command (for example the npx/node tool the server uses) or correct the server's command in its MCP configuration (claude mcp / .mcp.json).
rules.sourceStatement: Stdio servers run as local processes on your machine. They're ideal for tools that need direct system access or custom scripts.

rules.title: Restore or remove a missing hook script
rules.advice: A hook in your settings points at a script file that does not exist. Hooks run automatically at lifecycle events, so this hook will error every time its event fires.
rules.fix: Restore the script at the configured path, or delete that hook entry from settings.json.
rules.sourceStatement: Hooks are user-defined shell commands, HTTP endpoints, or LLM prompts that execute automatically at specific points in Claude Code's lifecycle.

rules.title: Install a missing hook command
rules.advice: A hook's external command could not be resolved on PATH when probed. If it is also missing in Claude Code's environment, the hook will fail whenever its event fires (PATH can differ between environments).
rules.fix: Install the command or fix your PATH so the executable resolves; alternatively point the hook at an absolute path, or remove the hook entry.
rules.sourceStatement: Exec form runs when args is present. Claude Code resolves command as an executable on PATH and spawns it directly with args as the argument vector.

rules.title: Fix a hook script's syntax error
rules.advice: A Node.js hook script fails to parse, so the hook command will exit with an error every time its lifecycle event fires instead of doing its job.
rules.fix: Fix the script until `node --check <file>` passes, or remove the hook from settings.json.
rules.sourceStatement: Exit 0 means success. [...] Any other exit code is a non-blocking error for most hook events. The transcript shows a <hook name> hook error notice followed by the first line of stderr.

rules.title: Repair a broken settings.json
rules.advice: A settings file is malformed or contains duplicate keys. settings.json is how Claude Code is configured, so broken JSON means some or all of your settings are dropped or applied unpredictably (for duplicate keys, the last value wins).
rules.fix: Edit the file at the reported line and column until it is valid JSON, then verify the result with claude-mgr `config show-effective`.
rules.sourceStatement: The settings.json file is the official mechanism for configuring Claude Code through hierarchical settings. User settings are defined in ~/.claude/settings.json and apply to all projects.

rules.title: Clean up old CLAUDE.md backups
rules.advice: Several CLAUDE.md.backup.* copies have accumulated in ~/.claude. Claude Code loads instructions from the live CLAUDE.md locations only — backup copies are not among the documented memory locations and just clutter the directory.
rules.fix: Review the backups and delete the ones you no longer need; keep the live ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md as the single curated copy.
rules.sourceStatement: CLAUDE.md files are loaded into the context window at the start of every session [...] User instructions: ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md — personal preferences for all projects.

rules.title: Fix the status line command
rules.advice: Your configured statusLine command could not be resolved, so the status line cannot render. The status line runs the shell script you configure and displays whatever it prints.
rules.fix: Point statusLine.command in settings.json at an existing script (and make it executable), or remove the statusLine setting.
rules.sourceStatement: The status line is a customizable bar at the bottom of Claude Code that runs any shell script you configure. [...] Set type to "command" and point command to a script path or an inline shell command.

rules.title: Put the claude CLI on your PATH
rules.advice: The `claude` command could not be resolved or did not respond on this machine. That usually means the install directory is missing from your PATH or an installation is broken.
rules.fix: Add the install directory (~/.local/bin on macOS/Linux, %USERPROFILE%\.local\bin on Windows) to PATH, keep only one installation, and verify with `claude --version`.
rules.sourceStatement: If installation succeeded but you get a command not found or not recognized error when running claude, the install directory isn't in your PATH. [...] the installer places claude at ~/.local/bin/claude or %USERPROFILE%\.local\bin\claude.exe.
